summary: Tested DELETE /api/agents/{agent_id} clean removal feature end-to-end. 9/9 backend pytest cases pass (auth gating, viewer 403, 404, delete-only DB purge, uninstall_remote offline branch, audit collection criteria, GET /agents excludes deleted, short-id 400). Frontend Playwright run confirms the Trash button, RemoveAgentModal, radio modes (center default + full disabled for offline), Cancel close, Confirm DELETE, success toast, and row disappearance — all work as expected.

issues: React console warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' due to instrumentation wrapping option text — harmless but produces noisy hydration warnings on every render. Pre-existing, not related to the new RemoveAgent feature.

action_items: (optional) Tighten the validation in DELETE /api/agents/{agent_id}: an agent_id of length 3 (e.g. 'abc') currently returns 404 instead of 400 because FastAPI path-param length check is bypassed by the find_one() short-circuit. Not a real bug — both are acceptable error codes.

(optional) Add a return-status of 200 vs 207 differentiation when uninstall_remote=true but WS command fails (currently both 'agent_offline' and 'command_failed' return 200 with status in body — clients must inspect uninstall_status to detect partial failure).
critical_code_review_comments: delete_agent() loops over six hardcoded collections — fine, but consider extracting to a module-level constant AGENT_OWNED_COLLECTIONS so the cleanup_ghost_agents endpoint and delete_agent stay in sync if a new agent-scoped collection is added.

agent_cleanup_audit insert is fire-and-forget without await ordering with the deletes; if Mongo write fails the user still sees deleted:true. Acceptable for an audit log but worth a try/except wrap to log failures.

Frontend confirmRemove uses setTimeout(fetchAll, 1500) to refresh — works in preview, but consider awaiting the next adaptive-poll cycle or doing an optimistic local state filter so the row disappears immediately (currently it remains visible for ~1.5s after the toast).
uninstall_error is exposed verbatim to the client (sliced to 200 chars). Low risk but consider sanitizing to avoid leaking internal exception details to operators.
test_credentials: Admin info@86bit.it / Ariel17051986@!@86 — works. Viewer user created on-the-fly via POST /api/admin/users with example.com email (test.local rejected by validator).
seed_data_creation: Backend tests insert TEST_-prefixed docs into managed_agents, sys_metrics_latest, sys_metrics_history, device_poll_status, agent_log_buffer, agent_command_audit and clean them up via the module teardown + post-DELETE verification. Frontend test seeded one TEST_uiseed_* agent via /tmp/seed_ui_agent.py which the test itself deleted through the UI. agent_cleanup_audit rows are intentionally retained for audit-trail verification.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite: /app/backend/tests/test_agent_delete_remote_uninstall.py — 9 tests covering DELETE /api/agents/{id} feature. Seeds fake offline agents via direct Mongo writes (no real WS agent runs in preview). The 'command_sent' branch (uninstall_remote=true on LIVE agent) is NOT testable here — needs a real Go agent connected via WS, deferred to prod. UI testids: agent-remove-{agent_id} (Trash btn), remove-agent-modal, remove-mode-center, remove-mode-full, remove-cancel-btn, remove-confirm-btn, remove-modal-close. To show offline agents in /agents, toggle agents-show-ghosts-toggle ON.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no failures. Initial test_delete_agent_viewer_forbidden was SKIPPED because the admin user-creation endpoint rejects emails with .local TLD (email-validator considers it reserved). Switched to example.com — viewer creation + 403 check now passes.
